name: Lagunas y Albardinales del Cigüela – circular from Quintanar de la Orden
name_original: Lagunas y Albardinales del Cigüela – circular desde Quintanar de la Orden
region_intro: Road cycling around Quintanar De La Orden features routes through the flat and gently rolling plains of La Mancha, characterized by extensive agricultural fields and open landscapes. The region's terrain is generally low in elevation, with gradual ascents and descents, making it suitable for sustained road cycling. Key features include historical windmills and natural areas like lagoons and salt marshes. The routes primarily traverse rural roads connecting small towns and villages. Best road cycling routes around Quintanar De La Orden The most popular road cycling route is Lagunas y Albardinales del Cigüela – circular from Quintanar de la Orden, a 65.4 miles (105.3 km) trail that takes 4 hours 5 minutes to complete. This route explores the natural lagoons and salt marshes of the Cigüela area. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is La Mancha de Toledo – circular from Quintanar de la Orden via Lillo and Corral de Almaguer, a moderate 44.8 miles (72.1 km) path. This route offers views of the classic La Mancha landscape, passing through agricultural lands and small towns. Local road cyclists also love the Molinos en Mota del Cuervo — circular por Quintanar de la Orden y Hontanaya, a 53.1 miles (85.5 km) trail leading through the iconic windmill landscape, often completed in about 2 hours 43 minutes. Road cycling around Quintanar De La Orden is defined by open agricultural plains, historic windmills, and natural lagoon areas. The network offers moderate challenges suitable for experienced road cyclists. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 40 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Quintanar De La Orden's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Quintanar De La Orden?
A: The region features flat and gently rolling plains, characterized by extensive agricultural fields and open landscapes. The terrain generally has low elevation with gradual ascents and descents, making it suitable for sustained road cycling. You'll primarily traverse rural roads connecting small towns and villages.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly road cycling routes in Quintanar De La Orden?
A: All the road cycling routes listed for Quintanar De La Orden are classified as moderate difficulty. While they don't feature extreme climbs, their distances (ranging from 44.8 to 65.4 miles) and open nature make them more suitable for experienced road cyclists rather than beginners or young families.

Q: What are some of the longer road cycling routes available?
A: For those seeking longer rides, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1023060951/lagunas-y-albardinales-del-cigueela-circular-from-quintanar-de-la-orden" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagunas y Albardinales del Cigüela – circular from Quintanar de la Orden&lt;/a&gt; route covers 65.4 miles (105.3 km), exploring natural lagoons and salt marshes. Another substantial option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20134693" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villamayor de Santiago – From Almendros to Casas de Lujan loop from Villanueva de Alcardete&lt;/a&gt;, which is 87.6 km long.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the routes around Quintanar De La Orden are circular. Popular options include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1023060951/lagunas-y-albardinales-del-cigueela-circular-from-quintanar-de-la-orden" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagunas y Albardinales del Cigüela – circular from Quintanar de la Orden&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1023081461/la-mancha-de-toledo-circular-from-quintanar-de-la-orden-via-lillo-and-corral-de-almaguer" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mancha de Toledo – circular from Quintanar de la Orden via Lillo and Corral de Almaguer&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1108819128/molinos-en-mota-del-cuervo-circular-por-quintanar-de-la-orden-y-hontanaya" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molinos en Mota del Cuervo — circular por Quintanar de la Orden y Hontanaya&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on these road cycling routes?
A: You'll experience the classic La Mancha landscape, characterized by vast agricultural fields, open skies, and rural villages. Many routes also feature historical windmills, particularly the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1108819128/molinos-en-mota-del-cuervo-circular-por-quintanar-de-la-orden-y-hontanaya" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molinos en Mota del Cuervo&lt;/a&gt; route, and natural areas like lagoons and salt marshes, as seen on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1023060951/lagunas-y-albardinales-del-cigueela-circular-from-quintanar-de-la-orden" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagunas y Albardinales del Cigüela&lt;/a&gt; route.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or attractions to see along the routes?
A: While cycling, you can encounter several points of interest. The region is known for its historic windmills. You might also pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4154343" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of the Hermitage of Santa Ana&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5331395" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Statue of Quixote and Dulcinea&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5449543" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quintanar de la Orden Town Center&lt;/a&gt; itself offers historical architecture.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Quintanar De La Orden?
A: Spring (April-May) and Autumn (September-October) generally offer the most pleasant conditions for road cycling in La Mancha, with milder temperatures. Summers can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon rides preferable, while winters are typically mild but can be windy.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Quintanar De La Orden?
A: There are 5 curated road cycling routes available in Quintanar De La Orden, all of which are classified as moderate difficulty and offer a great way to explore the region's unique landscape.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Quintanar De La Orden?
A: Over 40 road cyclists have explored Quintanar De La Orden using komoot. They often appreciate the open agricultural plains, the unique historical windmills, and the natural lagoon areas that define the region's cycling experience. The routes offer moderate challenges suitable for experienced riders.

Q: Is there parking available near the road cycling routes?
A: While specific parking areas are not detailed for each route, as the routes primarily traverse rural roads connecting small towns and villages, you can typically find parking in the town centers or designated public parking areas within Quintanar De La Orden or the other villages along the routes.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through specific towns or villages?
A: Yes, routes often connect various local towns. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1023081461/la-mancha-de-toledo-circular-from-quintanar-de-la-orden-via-lillo-and-corral-de-almaguer" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mancha de Toledo&lt;/a&gt; route passes through Lillo and Corral de Almaguer, offering glimpses into local life and architecture.
tips: Located in Plaza La Glorieta. Sculpture by Miguel de Cervantes, very similar to the one found in Puerto Lápice.

The Laguna Grande de Villafranca de los Caballeros is one of the largest river lagoons in La Mancha and the only one in the region with recreational use. With its 2.5 km of beach, it is a popular tourist destination, especially during the summer months.
In 2014, a beautiful urban art initiative was carried out inviting different artists to paint gigantic murals related to Don Quixote on the bare party walls.
